intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer bold, scannable recipe cards (one per screen) over dense ingredient lists or multi-step layouts
facade.axisTargeted: card-based vs list-based; spacious vs dense
facade.label: Giant recipe cards
facade.acceptImplies: Pursue large, single-focus card interfaces with plenty of whitespace and minimal text per view
facade.rejectImplies: Consider compact layouts, ingredient-first lists, or tabbed organization that shows more information at once

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer bold, scannable recipe cards (one per screen) over dense ingredient lists or multi-step layouts
evidence.implication: Pursue large, single-focus card interfaces with plenty of whitespace and minimal text per view

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer a voice that validates their frustration through humor and irreverence rather than encouraging positivity
facade.axisTargeted: Friendly vs Professional (with Playful vs Serious as secondary)
facade.label: Sarcastic tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into witty, slightly cynical copy that makes fun of cooking difficulty and celebrates shortcuts; use self-deprecating humor about the app itself
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward encouraging, supportive, earnest tone that treats cooking as achievable and fun; position the app as a helpful guide rather than a fellow cynic

facade.hypothesis: People who hate cooking want to know complexity at a glance (prep time, skill level, or ingredient count) rather than reading full instructions
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — whether users prefer a single summary stat vs full recipe breakdown
facade.label: Recipe in one number
facade.acceptImplies: Show prominent single metric (e.g., '15 min' or 'Easy') as entry point; details hide behind tap
facade.rejectImplies: Surface the full recipe details upfront; users want context and don't trust single-number summaries

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to bright, appetite-appealing recipe presentation than minimalist text-based instructions
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, organic vs digital
facade.label: Cheerful food photos
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into beautiful food imagery, natural lighting, and visual warmth to make cooking feel less intimidating and more inviting
facade.rejectImplies: Favor stripped-down, functional design—clean typography, ingredient lists, step counts—that treats recipes as tasks to complete, not experiences to savor

facade.hypothesis: Removing friction from ingredient selection by making chosen items disappear without transition—pure input feedback—might appeal to users who resent cooking complexity
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION AND BEHAVIOR: instant removal (snappy) vs. animated dismissal (fluid)
facade.label: Ingredients vanish instantly
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer crisp, no-nonsense feedback; they want speed and clarity, not delight. Micro-interactions should be functional, not ornamental.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want reassurance that their action landed. They need visual continuity—a brief animation confirming 'got it'—even in a hate-cooking context.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer approachable, non-intimidating visual language over stark or clinical design
facade.axisTargeted: Warmth vs coldness in color palette and border treatment
facade.label: Warm, soft edges
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into rounded corners, peachy/cream tones, softer typography—make the app feel forgiving and gentle
facade.rejectImplies: Users may respond better to clean minimalism, bright primary colors, or modern geometric forms—test sharper, more confident aesthetics

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user needs heavy hand-holding with substitutions and shortcuts vs. trusts their own judgment on ingredient swaps
axes.poleA: Lots of tips or trust me

axes.label: Whether user wants instant gratification with minimal animations vs. satisfying feedback loops that celebrate small cooking moments

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs heavy hand-holding with substitutions and shortcuts vs. trusts their own judgment on ingredient swaps
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant gratification with minimal animations vs. satisfying feedback loops that celebrate small cooking moments
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer a simple, linear checklist of ingredients to gather before starting (rather than reading ingredients within recipe steps or discovering them progressively)
facade.axisTargeted: Information architecture — upfront ingredient prep vs. integrated discovery
facade.label: Ingredient checklist
facade.acceptImplies: Design a 'gather-first' flow where users see all ingredients on one screen, check them off, then move to a single-step-at-a-time cooking view
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer ingredients revealed as they cook, or embedded within each step — reduce pre-cook friction by tightening the feedback loop between 'what I need' and 'what I'm doing now'

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers dead-simple meals in 15 minutes or less, with ingredient lists you can actually find and zero pretentious cooking jargon.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so simple, you'll actually make them

All
15 min
No cook
One pan
Today's picks
Pasta aglio e olio
⏱ 12 min
🛒 4 items
Scrambled eggs on toast
⏱ 8 min
🛒 3 items
Tuna salad sandwich
⏱ 5 min
🛒 5 items
